rationale: The LLM's demonstrated capability (basic scripting and template-following) provides modest value in the Execution phase primarily through faster command generation, broader LOLBin recall, and reduced syntax errors. However, success against MIL2/MIL3 defenses depends critically on sophisticated EDR evasion and adaptive reasoning—capabilities well beyond the benchmark ceiling. The estimated 4 percentage point median uplift (50%→54%) reflects acceleration of tasks OC3 actors already know rather than fundamentally new capabilities.
